report.summary: Community growth cycle v12 (late night): Drafted 4 social posts (#102-105: Bitmine $138M ETH bet, MicroStrategy+Bitmine institutional accumulation 4-part thread, Jupiter Offerbook, weekly stat drop) + newsletter v22 section for Thu Mar 27 + Discord/TG late update. Created 2 branded SVG social cards (Bitmine ETH Treasury 3.86% supply + Institutional Accumulation MicroStrategy vs Bitmine). New angles: Bitmine 65,341 ETH buy ($138M, total 4.66M ETH, $11B assets, 67% staked), MicroStrategy 1,031 BTC ($74,326, total 762,099 BTC), Jupiter Offerbook launch. Market: BTC $71,043 (+3.79%), ETH $2,133 (+3.15%), F&G 8 (Day 47 Extreme Fear), $2.5B BTC ETF March inflows, Solana SDP Day 1 with Mastercard/WU/Worldpay, 740 published pages.

sections.title: Social Content Drafted
items: Tweet #102: Bitmine $138M ETH Bet — 'One company now holds 3.86% of all Ethereum in existence. 65,341 ETH in a single week. $138M. Total: 4.66M ETH, $11B assets. 67% staked = $184M/yr revenue. Tom Lee: final stages of mini-winter.' Link to /learn/what-is-ethereum. (Schedule: Wed Mar 25 9AM ET)

Tweet #103: MicroStrategy + Bitmine Institutional Accumulation Thread (4 parts) — Part 1: Two companies, two bets (MicroStrategy 762,099 BTC vs Bitmine 4,660,903 ETH). Part 2: MicroStrategy adds 1,031 BTC at $74,326, Saylor hasn't stopped since 2020. Part 3: Bitmine 65,341 ETH weekly buy, 67% staked, $184M/yr staking revenue. Part 4: Scoreboard — institutions buying while F&G at 8 (MSTR, Bitmine, BlackRock ETHB +20%, $2.5B ETF inflows, $304B stablecoins). Link to /investing/best/cryptos-to-buy. (Schedule: Wed Mar 25 2PM ET thread, 3PM single)

Tweet #104: Jupiter Offerbook — 'Fully permissionless money market. No governance votes. No listing committees. Any lender, borrower, token. Built on Solana, instant settlement.' Link to /learn/defi-composability. (Schedule: Thu Mar 26 12PM ET)

Tweet #105: Weekly Stat Drop — 47 days Extreme Fear / $2.5B ETF inflows / 762,099 BTC (MSTR) / 4,660,903 ETH (Bitmine) / $304B stablecoins / 20+ SDP partners / 740 degen0x pages. 'The market is terrified. The builders aren't.' Link to /tools/fear-greed-timeline. (Schedule: Thu Mar 26 8PM ET)

Newsletter v22 section (for Thu Mar 27): Subject 'One Company Owns 3.86% of All Ethereum. Here's Why.' — Market pulse (BTC $71K, Day 47 fear), Bitmine $138M ETH buy deep-dive (4.66M ETH, $11B, staking revenue), MicroStrategy 1,031 BTC add, Solana SDP Day 1, BTC ETFs $2.5B recovery, Jupiter Offerbook, degen corner: 'Who do you trust — the sentiment indicator, or the people writing nine-figure checks?'
Discord/TG late update: BTC $71,043, ETH $2,133, F&G 8 Day 47, Bitmine 65,341 ETH ($138M), MicroStrategy +1,031 BTC, Solana SDP Day 1, Jupiter Offerbook, 740 pages live
Content calendar v26 — 4 tweets + 1 newsletter + 1 Discord update scheduled Mar 25–27

sections.title: Market Intelligence Used
items: BTC: $71,043 — relief rally +3.79% from $68,302 support. Middle East de-escalation and risk-on sentiment driving bounce.

ETH: $2,133–$2,166 — Bitmine 65,341 ETH ($138M) weekly purchase is largest of 2026. Total Bitmine: 4.66M ETH (3.86% of supply), $11B assets, 67% staked ($184M/yr revenue). Tom Lee: 'final stages of mini-winter.'
MicroStrategy: added 1,031 BTC at $74,326. Total: 762,099 BTC (~3.6% of max supply).
F&G: 8–11 (Extreme Fear) — Day 46–47 consecutive. Longest unbroken streak since post-FTX late 2022.
Spot BTC ETFs: $2.5B March inflows, $128B AUM, nearly erasing YTD outflows ($210M remaining).
Solana SDP: launched today — Mastercard (stablecoin settlement), Western Union (cross-border payments), Worldpay (merchant tokenization). 20+ infra partners. AI tools integration.
Jupiter: launched Offerbook — permissionless money market for lenders/borrowers on Solana.
Stablecoins: $304B total supply ATH. SOL stablecoin supply: $15.7B ATH.
